name: Gretsch Golf Academy
address: 8580 Dean Martin Dr, Las Vegas, NV 89139
about: Gretsch Golf Academy is a premier indoor golf training facility located in Las Vegas, NV. The academy features 13 golf bays with Foresight Falcon simulators, PGA Professional instruction, and a dedicated short game area with a real sand bunker. It offers a comprehensive training environment focused on skill development, utilizing modern technology such as Swing Catalyst video analysis and providing access to 1,800+ virtual golf courses. The facility is designed for structured practice, private lessons, club fittings, and membership-based programs catering to golfers of all ages and skill levels.
why_golfers_like_it: High-end Foresight Falcon simulators with precise shot data and video analysis
Dedicated short game area with real sand bunker for authentic practice conditions
Female-led, professional PGA instruction with a focus on long-term development
Two private bays with curved screens and lounge seating for immersive group sessions
16,000 sq ft purpose-built training facility designed for focused improvement

food_and_drink.notes: No food service is mentioned. Outside food may be allowed.
A non-alcoholic refreshment bar is available in private bays. No BYOB policy is stated.

simulator_hardware.notes: 9 standard bays and 2 private bays with precise ball flight tracking and data analysis

faq.question: Do I need to bring my own clubs?
faq.answer: Yes, you are expected to bring your own clubs for use in the simulator bays and short game area.

faq.question: Can I bring my own food and drink?
faq.answer: No external food or alcohol is allowed, though a non-alcoholic refreshment bar is available in private bays.

faq.question: How long does it take to play 18 holes?
faq.answer: Play times can vary, but a full 18-hole round typically takes 2 to 3 hours depending on the number of players and pace of play.

faq.question: Is the facility kid-friendly?
faq.answer: Yes, the academy has a multi-level Junior Academy for beginners and competitive juniors, making it a great environment for young golfers to learn and develop their skills.
